fr.elements: Tennis Court; Outdoor Gathering Area; Vehicle Parking Area; Landscaped Area; Pedestrian Walkways
fr.recommendations: Ensuring clear markings and signage for the multiple access points could facilitate rapid navigation for emergency responders upon arrival. Maintaining unobstructed pedestrian walkways and vehicle parking areas would support smooth movement and equipment placement during emergency operations. It may be beneficial to designate specific areas within the parking and outdoor gathering spaces for emergency vehicle staging, ensuring that they remain free from obstructions. Furthermore, managing the landscaped areas to prevent potential encroachment onto pathways or access routes could enhance visibility and accessibility, thereby supporting operational efficiency. Ensuring that the tennis court and outdoor gathering area boundaries are clearly delineated would assist responders in quickly identifying key site features during an emergency.

wildfire.conditions: Scattered Trees; Open Green Space; Structure Cluster; Vegetation Adjacent to Structure; Scattered Shrubs
wildfire.recommendations: Enhancing defensible space and reducing fuel continuity could involve managing vegetation that is adjacent to structures by ensuring adequate separation from combustible materials and maintaining an open space buffer around the structures. This can be achieved by creating defensible perimeters to limit the spread of fire into clustered structures. Managing scattered shrubs and maintaining the open green spaces can help to limit fuel continuity. Preservation and possible expansion of these open areas will not only support defensive actions but also enhance long-term fire resilience. The retention of these open spaces can also serve as effective fuel breaks, which are crucial in allowing safer access and operations for firefighting efforts. Regular inspection and maintenance of these landscapes can further enhance property preparedness and ensure ongoing protection against wildfire exposure.
